title: Query Optimization Polystore Framework
summary: A query optimization framework inspired by three foundational papers in database systems. Apache Calcite introduces a modular, extensible query processing framework with a Volcano-style cost-based optimizer, relational algebra, SQL parsing, and an adapter architecture for heterogeneous data sources. Polypheny-DB extends this with a polystore model combining multiple data store types (relational, document, key-value), cost-based query routing, data partitioning, and adaptive data placement. Orca (GPORCA) adds a distributed MPP query optimizer with DXL intermediate representation, memo-based plan enumeration, transformation/implementation rules, and distributed cost modeling.
instructionPreview: Implement a query optimization, polystore, and distributed MPP optimizer framework in Python. The project has three modules to implement in order:
instruction:
Implement a query optimization, polystore, and distributed MPP optimizer framework in Python.
The project has three modules to implement in order:

1. **Apache Calcite Query Optimizer** (calcite): Implement relational algebra operators
   (TableScan, Filter, Project, Join, Aggregate, Sort), a Volcano-style cost-based optimizer,
   optimization rules (filter pushdown, join reordering, projection merging, filter merging),
   a SQL parser, and a plan executor. Run experiments measuring optimization effectiveness.

2. **Polypheny-DB Polystore** (polypheny): Extend with multiple data store adapters
   (relational, document, key-value), cost-based query routing, hash/range data partitioning
   with partition pruning, federated cross-store query execution, and workload-driven
   data placement. Run experiments measuring federation and partitioning effectiveness.

3. **Orca MPP Query Optimizer** (orca): Implement a distributed query optimizer with DXL
   intermediate representation, memo-based plan enumeration (groups/group expressions),
   transformation rules (join commutativity, associativity, predicate pushdown, aggregate
   splitting), implementation rules (hash join, merge join, broadcast/redistribute motion),
   a cost model for MPP execution (network cost, skew estimation), statistics derivation
   (histograms, NDV, join cardinality), multi-stage optimization, and property enforcement.
   Run experiments measuring optimization quality and cost model accuracy.

Working directory: /workspace
Test command: pytest /tests/<module>/test_outputs.py -rA
Each module adds files and updates run.sh. See requirements/<module>.yaml for details.

moduleDag.description: A query optimization framework inspired by three foundational papers in database systems. Apache Calcite introduces a modular, extensible query processing framework with a Volcano-style cost-based optimizer, relational algebra, SQL parsing, and an adapter architecture for heterogeneous data sources. Polypheny-DB extends this with a polystore model combining multiple data store types (relational, document, key-value), cost-based query routing, data partitioning, and adaptive data placement. Orca (GPORCA) adds a distributed MPP query optimizer with DXL intermediate representation, memo-based plan enumeration, transformation/implementation rules, and distributed cost modeling.

nodes.label: Apache Calcite Query Optimizer
nodes.description: Implements the core query optimization framework: relational algebra operators (TableScan, Filter, Project, Join, Aggregate, Sort), a Volcano-style cost-based planner with equivalence sets, optimization rules (filter pushdown, join reordering, projection merging), a SQL parser, and a plan executor.

nodes.label: Polypheny-DB Polystore
nodes.description: Extends the Calcite optimizer with polystore capabilities: multiple data store adapters (relational, document, key-value) with different cost models, a cost-based query router, hash and range data partitioning with partition-aware scanning, federated cross-store query execution, and workload-driven data placement optimization.

nodes.label: Orca/GPORCA: MPP Query Optimizer
nodes.description: Distributed MPP query optimizer with DXL (Data eXchange Language) intermediate representation, memo structure with groups and group expressions for plan space enumeration, transformation rules (join commutativity, associativity, predicate pushdown, aggregate splitting), implementation rules (hash join, merge join, broadcast/redistribute motion), cost model for distributed execution with network cost and skew estimation, statistics derivation (histograms, NDV, join cardinality), multi-stage optimization (exploration, implementation, optimization), and property enforcement (distribution, sort order, partitioning).

edges.label: Polypheny-DB uses Calcite as query optimization framework
Orca extends Calcite's optimization framework for distributed MPP queries
